ogTitle: Woodville West vs Woolpunda: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Woodville West and Woolpunda.
heading: Woodville West vs Woolpunda — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Woodville West, South Australia and Woolpunda, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Woolpunda has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Woodville West (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Woodville West or Woolpunda safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Woodville West has a flood risk score of 46/100, while Woolpunda scores 33/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Woodville West scores 47/100 for bushfire risk. Woolpunda scores 40/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Woodville West or Woolpunda?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Woolpunda has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.52% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
